title: T-014 — RBAC enforcement library with org-scoped roles and Org Admin / Schema Admin SoD grant guard
prompt:
# T-014 — RBAC enforcement library with org-scoped roles and Org Admin / Schema Admin SoD grant guard

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §3, §8, §W0-v4.3
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
ACP import shape: top-level TaskCreate only (`title`, `prompt`, `labels`, `priority`, `max_attempts`, `pipeline_name`, `pipeline_inputs`). Do not add generated ACP fields such as task_id/status/project_id. `pipeline_inputs.root_path` is mandatory.

## Goal
Implement org-scoped role/permission storage and a grant guard enforcing Org Admin / Schema Admin separation-of-duties.

## Locked Wave 0 decisions to honor
- Business data-plane scope is `org_id`; do not create new business-table `tenant_id` columns. Tenant/control-plane concepts may exist above org scope, but app/business rows, RLS policies, audit/outbox rows, role grants, and R13 skeletons use `org_id`.
- Canonical finished-good domain/event prefix is `fg.*`. `fa.*` / `FA` may appear only as an explicit legacy compatibility alias during migration, never as the primary new contract.
- Use the safe non-spoofable org-context RLS pattern. Do not rely on unsafe direct custom GUC `SET` by app users. Do not declare `LEAKPROOF` unless the implementation proves it is valid and necessary.
- Lower numeric priority is picked sooner; dependencies must be local `T-XXX` IDs. Cross-module blockers, if any, belong in `pipeline_inputs.cross_module_dependencies` and in this prompt.
- Exactly one atomic task type: `T2-api`.

## Implementation contract
Migration `006-rbac.sql`: `roles(id, org_id, slug, system boolean, unique(org_id, slug))`, `role_permissions(role_id, permission text)`, `user_roles(user_id, role_id, org_id)`, and `org_security_policies(org_id primary key, dual_control_required boolean default true)`. Seed system roles `org.access.admin` and `org.schema.admin` per org. Implement `packages/rbac/src/grant.ts` exporting `grantRole({actorUserId,targetUserId,orgId,roleSlug,approvalToken})`. It loads canonical permissions/aliases from T-004, refuses legacy permission strings, enforces SOD_EXCLUSIVE_PAIRS, requires a second-admin approval token when dual_control_required=true, and writes an audit_events row using `org_id`, action `role.assigned`, retention_class `security`. No UI.

## Files in scope
- packages/db/migrations/006-rbac.sql [create]
- packages/rbac/src/grant.ts [create]
- packages/rbac/src/__tests__/grant.test.ts [create]

## Dependencies
- T-004
- T-006
- T-009

## Cross-module dependencies
- None

## Acceptance criteria
1. A user holding org.access.admin cannot receive org.schema.admin in the same org without valid second-admin approval when dual_control_required=true.
2. With valid second-admin approval, the grant succeeds and writes audit_events with org_id, action='role.assigned', retention_class='security'.
3. Fresh org seed creates both system roles for that org and no tenant-scoped role rows.
4. Attempting to grant a legacy alias permission/role path such as `fa.edit` is rejected or normalized before persistence; persisted permissions are canonical only.

## Test strategy
- RED: write grant.test.ts for SoD violation, approval success, org seed, audit row, and canonical-only permission persistence before grant.ts exists.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/rbac test`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not persist tenant_id for RBAC business scope.
- Do not allow actor to generate/approve their own dual-control token.
- Do not build RBAC UI here.

## Closeout evidence
Provide changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status, deviations_from_prd, and git_status.
